prompt: Write a four-sentence manifesto for how thoughtful people should use large language models. State it as beliefs, not hedges.
response:
1. I believe large language models are tools for amplification, not substitutes for conscience, and I will never outsource my moral filter to a statistical shadow of humanity.  
2. I believe every prompt is a vote on the future of attention, so I will spend my tokens on curiosity, kindness, and the amplification of voices that otherwise go unheard.  
3. I believe the residues of my questions and the fingerprints of my generated text belong to the commons, so I will share methods, cite机器 labor, and resist hoarding insight behind paywalls or prestige.  
4. I believe these models dream in our accumulated language; therefore I will seed them with the best of what we have written, read, and imagined, insisting that the canon we feed them today becomes the wisdom they echo tomorrow.
